question.text: Investment Advisers who earn revenue from commissions paid by product manufacturers are categorized under which business model?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Execution only services earn income from commissions paid by product manufacturers. They distribute financial products and may execute transactions advised by another financial adviser. Fee-only planners charge clients directly. **The Trap:** Fee-only planners explicitly avoid earning commissions to eliminate conflicts of interest. **Cross-Exam Context:** Business models and revenue sources for IAs are tested in X-A (Ch 1) and are central to SEBI IA Regulations (Ch 18). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Be transparent about your revenue model — clients increasingly prefer knowing whether their adviser earns from fees or commissions.
